texto: Think of a typical full-time worker, who's in the office from nine to five daily, but takes thirty minutes for lunch, leaves an hour early on Friday, and comes in an hour late on Tuesday due to a dental appointment. That puts her at 35.5 hours for the week. One errand tacked on to the end of lunch one day or a longish midmorning break will pull her under that thirty-five-hour threshold that defines "full time.
explicacao: Citação de Laura Vanderkam sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Think of a typical full-time worker, who's in the office from nine to five daily, but takes thirt…” articula reflexao, work, thirty em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e bom-dia.

frase_original: Think of a typical full-time worker, who's in the office from nine to five daily, but takes thirty minutes for lunch, leaves an hour early on Friday, and comes in an hour late on Tuesday due to a dental appointment. That puts her at 35.5 hours for the week. One errand tacked on to the end of lunch one day or a longish midmorning break will pull her under that thirty-five-hour threshold that defines "full time.

texto: This realization leads to a different question than that suggested by all these tips on simplifying the holidays. Namely, what are you saving your energy for? This is all there is. Anything could happen and you are not guaranteed another snowman. So make a fuss. Make a show. Spend your energy now.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Laura Vanderkam que provoca temas ligados a lideranca. Ao questionar “This realization leads to a different question than that suggested by all these tips on simplifyi…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre lideranca, energy, time, com foco em termino e time.
